mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering profit warnings and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.4pp, or the signal fails to predict direction in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and yielding positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both positive MoM
failure_condition: Joint positive MoM moves in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns (direction accuracy < 50%) over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals supply-side bottlenecks and margin compression, forcing equity repricing and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of periods where INDPRO falls below its 12-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading above 4.5% triggers immediate inflation risk premium repricing, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly gains in total nonfarm payrolls reflect robust economic activity and rising corporate payrolls, supporting earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS MoM changes against 60-day forward returns falls below 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly personal consumption expenditures, where RSAFS declines while GDPC1 remains elevated, signals unsustainable demand composition and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence condition fails to predict negative 20-day returns (accuracy < 45%) across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase signals expanding corporate capacity and improving profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive directional bias when INDPRO accelerates during post-recession periods (accuracy < 50%).
